title: Express 5 Resource Design, Middleware Order, Async Errors, and Cancellation · Inference Brief: Feed Ts Api Resource Decisions
summary: Routes mirror tables, middleware mutates unvalidated request objects, async failures fall through inconsistently, responses double-send, and disconnects do not cancel dependencies. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed ts api resource decisions increment in layered Express 5 feed-reader API. Learners progress…
objectives: Design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics.
Stop claims, abort fetches, await settlements, expire or finalize leases, clear timers and listeners, close the pool, and assert no unexpected handles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Api Resource Decisions model · Theory Feed Ts Ap…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Api Resource Decisions precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Api Resource Decisions model · Theory Feed Ts Ap…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 9820 during an inclusive cross-device research reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile resource decisions, middleware order, async errors, request abort, and response finality; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics.
